3.2  Threat Model
We assume that an attacker can perform the following oper
• A1: A mistakenly issued transaction by an authori
• A2: Tricking users to issue unwanted transactions
• A3: Steal user private keys and illegally execute tr
• A4: Exploit smart contract vulnerabilities.
Our model focuses on application-level attacks since the Hy
several external threats: Sybil attacks [21] by using permi
control participant identities; Network Partitioning [22] s
that valid transactions are eventually committed; Eclipse a

We also do not consider possible flaws in the underlying co
since delving into underlying technologies is out the scope
